action.label: Place Order
action.tooltip: Open a long or short position with specified size. Choose market, limit, or stop order type.

action.label: Close Position
action.tooltip: Close the current open position. Use with conditions to implement exit logic.

action.label: Close All
action.tooltip: Close all open positions at once. Useful for end-of-day or emergency exits.

action.label: Stop Loss
action.tooltip: Automatically exit if price moves against you. Essential for risk management.

action.label: Take Profit
action.tooltip: Automatically exit when price reaches profit target. Lock in gains.

action.label: Trailing Stop
action.tooltip: Stop that follows price movement. Locks in profits while allowing upside.

action.label: Notification
action.tooltip: Send alert via Telegram, Discord, or email when conditions are met.

action.label: Log
action.tooltip: Log a message to console for debugging and strategy analysis.

action.label: Options Order
action.tooltip: Place an order for options contracts. Used for hedging (protective puts, collars) or speculation.

action.label: Portfolio Rebalance
action.tooltip: Automatically buys and sells assets to restore the portfolio to its target allocation weights.

action.label: Phone Call
action.tooltip: AI realtime voice call (Gemini Live) to the user\'s phone, in-browser, or paired iOS app. Speaks the reason aloud and answers follow-up questions live with full tool access.

agent.label: News Analyst
agent.tooltip: Analyzes recent news headlines against your portfolio holdings. Uses LLM to assess sentiment, relevance, and impact. Outputs actionable signals (bullish/bearish) with confidence scores.

agent.label: Macro Analyst
agent.tooltip: Analyzes macroeconomic indicators (CPI, GDP, rates, employment) and their impact on your portfolio. Classifies the current macro regime and identifies risk/opportunity flags.

agent.label: Technical Analyst
agent.tooltip: Runs comprehensive technical analysis using indicators, candlestick patterns, support/resistance levels, and market regime classification. Provides directional signals with confidence.

agent.label: Social Monitor
agent.tooltip: Monitors social media feeds (Twitter/X, Reddit) for sentiment shifts and trending discussions about your portfolio holdings. Detects viral posts and sentiment extremes.

agent.label: Synthesis Agent
agent.tooltip: Combines outputs from multiple analyst agents into a unified recommendation. Weighs conflicting signals, identifies consensus, and produces a final actionable view with confidence scoring.

agent.label: Fundamentals Agent
agent.tooltip: Analyzes company fundamentals: financial ratios, growth metrics, profitability, and balance sheet health. Compares against peers and provides a financial health score.

agent.label: Sentiment Agent
agent.tooltip: Fuses sentiment from news, social media, and options flow into a composite sentiment score. Detects sentiment divergences and extreme readings that may signal turning points.

agent.label: Quant Agent
agent.tooltip: Quantitative agent with read access to Bloomberg-style terminal functions (HDS holders, DES description, GIP intraday graph, SPLC supply chain, WEI world indices, …). Pick which functions it can call; the agent fetches each one, synthesizes the data, and emits a signal with confidence.

agent.label: Research Agent
agent.tooltip: Runs quantitative research using the app\'s built-in tools: QuantStats analysis, Monte Carlo simulations, VaR/CVaR risk assessment, stress testing, and parameter optimization. Outputs data-driven insights to inform trading decisions.

condition.label: Compare
condition.tooltip: Compare A vs B using operators (>, <, ==, etc.). Returns true/false for use in If nodes.

condition.label: Crossover
condition.tooltip: Triggers when value A crosses above value B. Classic signal for MA crossover strategies.

condition.label: Crossunder
condition.tooltip: Triggers when value A crosses below value B. Used for exit signals or short entries.

condition.label: Threshold
condition.tooltip: Check if an indicator value is above or below a specific level (e.g., RSI > 70).

condition.label: In Range
condition.tooltip: Check if value falls between minimum and maximum bounds. Useful for RSI neutral zones.

condition.label: AND
condition.tooltip: Logical AND: Both conditions must be true. Combine multiple signals for confirmation.

condition.label: OR
condition.tooltip: Logical OR: Either condition being true triggers. Use for multiple entry criteria.

condition.label: NOT
condition.tooltip: Logical NOT: Inverts true to false and vice versa.

control.label: If
control.tooltip: Execute actions only when condition is true. Core building block for strategy logic.

control.label: If-Else
control.tooltip: Execute different actions based on condition. True path vs False path.

control.label: Repeat
control.tooltip: Repeat actions a specific number of times. Use for grid-based or pyramid entries.

control.label: Wait
control.tooltip: Pause strategy execution for specified seconds. Add time buffers.

control.label: Wait Until
control.tooltip: Pause until a condition becomes true. Wait for price levels or time.

control.label: Stop
control.tooltip: Immediately stop strategy execution. Emergency brake.

control.label: Switch
control.description: Route to N branches by case
control.tooltip: Evaluate an input value against N case expressions and route the signal to the matching output branch (or "Default" if none match). Inspired by n8n\'s Switch node — use when If/IfElse becomes a chain of nested branches.

control.label: Split In Batches
control.description: Iterate a list in batches of N
control.tooltip: Process a list of items (e.g. tickers from a screener) in batches. Each tick emits one batch of size `batchSize`; downstream nodes process the batch and the loop continues until the list is exhausted. Inspired by n8n\'s Split In Batches — distinct from Split Out which fans out one item per branch.

dataSource.label: Yahoo Finance
dataSource.tooltip: Fetches historical OHLCV from Yahoo Finance. Best for global stocks, ETFs, indices, and major crypto. Free, no API key.

dataSource.label: Avanza
dataSource.tooltip: Fetches OHLCV from Avanza's reverse-engineered web API. Best for Nordic listings (Stockholm, Copenhagen, Helsinki, Oslo). The orderbookId is auto-resolved by ticker or ISIN.

dataSource.label: Financial Modeling Prep
dataSource.tooltip: Fetches OHLCV from Financial Modeling Prep. Better fundamentals coverage than yfinance for US equities. Requires FMP_API_KEY env var.

dataSource.label: Avanza Positions
dataSource.tooltip: Streams the user\'s connected Avanza account positions into the flow. Useful for portfolio-aware risk overlays. Live-only; not backtestable.

dataSource.label: Avanza Watchlist
dataSource.description: Symbols from a saved Avanza watchlist
dataSource.tooltip: Iterates over the symbols in one of the user\'s saved Avanza watchlists. Useful as the symbol input for a multi-asset strategy. Live-only.

dataSource.label: FRED Macro
dataSource.tooltip: Fetches macro time-series from FRED (CPI, unemployment, fed funds, GDP, etc.). Use as a regime filter or macro-overlay input. Free with FRED_API_KEY.

environment.label: Current Price
environment.tooltip: Get current bid, ask, or mid price. Foundation for most trading logic.

environment.label: Bid-Ask Spread
environment.tooltip: Current spread in pips. Use to avoid trading during high-spread periods.

environment.label: Prev Open
environment.tooltip: Get the open price of a previous candle. Shift parameter controls how many bars back.

environment.label: Prev Close
environment.tooltip: Get the close price of a previous candle. Essential for confirmation signals.

environment.label: Current Time
environment.tooltip: Get current time. Use for session-based trading strategies.

environment.label: Day of Week
environment.tooltip: Get day of week (0=Sunday). Filter trades to specific days.

environment.label: New Candle
environment.tooltip: Triggers once when a new candle opens. Use to run logic only at bar close.

environment.label: Market Open
environment.tooltip: Check if market is currently open. Prevent trading during closed hours.

indicator.label: SMA
indicator.tooltip: Calculates the average price over N periods. Use to identify trend direction and potential support/resistance levels.

indicator.label: EMA
indicator.tooltip: Weighted average giving more importance to recent prices. More responsive to price changes than SMA.

indicator.label: DEMA
indicator.tooltip: Reduces lag by applying EMA twice. Faster response to price changes.

indicator.label: TEMA
indicator.tooltip: Triple smoothing for minimal lag. Best for fast-moving markets.

indicator.label: RSI
indicator.tooltip: Measures momentum on a 0-100 scale. Above 70 = overbought, below 30 = oversold.

indicator.label: Stochastic
indicator.tooltip: Compares closing price to price range. %K and %D lines signal momentum shifts.

indicator.label: CCI
indicator.tooltip: Measures deviation from average price. +100/-100 indicate overbought/oversold.

indicator.label: StochRSI
indicator.tooltip: Applies Stochastic formula to RSI values. Extremely sensitive oscillator for overbought/oversold.

indicator.label: MFI
indicator.tooltip: Volume-weighted RSI. Measures buying/selling pressure with volume confirmation.

indicator.label: MACD
indicator.tooltip: Shows relationship between two EMAs. Signal line crossovers indicate momentum shifts.

indicator.label: ADX
indicator.tooltip: Measures trend strength (not direction). Above 25 = strong trend, below 20 = weak/ranging.

indicator.label: Parabolic SAR
indicator.tooltip: Trailing stop indicator. Dots flip above/below price to signal trend reversals.

indicator.label: Ichimoku Cloud
indicator.tooltip: Displays 5 lines (Tenkan, Kijun, Span A/B, Chikou) to identify support/resistance and trend status.

indicator.label: Williams Alligator
indicator.tooltip: Uses 3 smoothed moving averages (Jaw, Teeth, Lips) to identify the absence of a trend or a trend in development.

indicator.label: Aroon
indicator.tooltip: Consists of two lines (Up and Down) to identify trend changes and strength.

indicator.label: DMI (+DI/-DI/ADX)
indicator.tooltip: Includes +DI, -DI, and ADX to determine both trend direction and strength.

indicator.label: Bollinger Bands
indicator.tooltip: Price bands based on standard deviation. Squeeze indicates low volatility, expansion signals breakout.

indicator.label: Keltner Channel
indicator.tooltip: Uses ATR for band width instead of standard deviation. Better for trending markets.

indicator.label: Donchian Channels
indicator.tooltip: Uses high/low of N periods to form upper/lower bands. Excellent for breakout strategies.

indicator.label: ATR
indicator.tooltip: Measures market volatility. Use for position sizing and stop-loss placement.

indicator.label: OBV
indicator.tooltip: Cumulative volume indicator. Rising OBV confirms uptrend, falling confirms downtrend.

indicator.label: VWAP
indicator.tooltip: Institutional benchmark price. Trading above = bullish, below = bearish.

indicator.label: HMM Regime
indicator.tooltip: Detects market regimes (e.g., bull/bear/sideways) using Hidden Markov Models.

indicator.label: Asset Spread
indicator.description: Price ratio or spread between two assets
indicator.tooltip: Calculates the spread (A - B) or ratio (A / B) for statistical arbitrage and pairs trading.

integration.label: Telegram
integration.tooltip: Send message or receive reply via Telegram bot. Use for alerts, HITL approvals, or collecting user input during workflow execution.

integration.label: Slack
integration.tooltip: Post message to a Slack channel or DM. Supports rich formatting with blocks, buttons, and attachments.

integration.label: Email
integration.tooltip: Send email notifications with HTML templates. Use for daily digests, weekly reports, or urgent alerts.

integration.label: SMS
integration.tooltip: Send SMS alerts to a phone number. Use for critical, time-sensitive alerts that require immediate attention.

integration.label: HTTP Request
integration.tooltip: Make HTTP requests to any REST API — the most versatile node. Fetch data, send commands, or integrate with any service that has an API. Inspired by n8n\'s HTTP Request node.

integration.label: Database Query
integration.tooltip: Execute a SQL query against the strategy database. Useful for fetching historical data, custom analytics, or writing computed results.

integration.label: Python Code
integration.tooltip: Execute custom Python code within the workflow. Code is sent to the Python compute service for execution. Access to pandas, numpy, ta-lib, and all Python libraries.

integration.label: JavaScript Code
integration.tooltip: Execute custom JavaScript code within the workflow. Runs in a sandboxed Node.js environment. Great for data transformation, calculations, and custom logic.

integration.label: AI Analysis
integration.tooltip: Send data to the Python ADK agent for AI-powered analysis. Used for sentiment analysis, regime detection, strategy recommendations, and complex decision-making.

integration.label: Merge
integration.tooltip: Combine outputs from multiple branches into one data stream. Modes: Append (concatenate), Join (match by key), or Wait (wait for all inputs). Inspired by n8n\'s Merge node.

integration.label: Split Out
integration.tooltip: Split one output to multiple branches. Each item in the input array is sent to a separate output. Useful for processing each ticker in a scan result individually. Inspired by n8n\'s Split Out node.

integration.label: Set
integration.tooltip: Set, rename, or delete fields on the data payload flowing through the workflow. Useful for reshaping data between nodes. Inspired by n8n\'s Set node.

integration.label: Filter
integration.tooltip: Pass through only items that match a condition. Items that don\'t match are discarded or sent to a "rejected" output. Useful for screening tickers or filtering signals. Inspired by n8n\'s Filter node.

integration.label: Aggregate
integration.tooltip: Aggregate multiple data items into summary statistics: sum, average, count, min, max. Useful for portfolio-level metrics or combining scanner results. Inspired by n8n\'s Aggregate node.

integration.label: Human Approval
integration.tooltip: Pauses workflow execution until a human approves or rejects via Telegram, Slack, or the web UI. The orchestrator holds state in Redis until the callback is received. Inspired by n8n\'s Human-in-the-Loop pattern.

integration.label: Sub-Workflow
integration.description: Execute another saved workflow as a reusable module
integration.tooltip: Runs a saved workflow as a black-box sub-process. Data from this node\'s inputs is passed as the trigger payload; the sub-workflow\'s final output is returned as this node\'s output. Equivalent to n8n\'s Execute Sub-workflow node.

llm.label: LLM Decision
llm.tooltip: Runs a prompt and returns a JSON decision object (e.g., {"shouldTrade": true}).

defaultData.label: LLM Decision
defaultData.prompt: Decide whether to enter a trade based on context.

llm.label: Sentiment Analysis
llm.tooltip: Analyzes news articles, social media, or custom text to determine market sentiment (-1 bearish to +1 bullish).

defaultData.label: Sentiment Analysis
defaultData.prompt: Analyze the following market news/text and determine the overall sentiment. Return a score from -1 (very bearish) to +1 (very bullish).

llm.label: Regime Detection
llm.tooltip: Uses LLM to analyze price action and indicators to classify the current market regime (trending, ranging, volatile).

defaultData.label: Regime Detection
defaultData.prompt: Analyze the provided market data and indicators. Classify the current market regime as one of: trending_up, trending_down, ranging, or volatile.

llm.label: NL Strategy Rules
llm.tooltip: Define trading rules in plain English. The LLM interprets them and generates trading signals.

defaultData.label: NL Strategy Rules
defaultData.prompt: Buy when price is near support and RSI is oversold. Sell when price reaches resistance or RSI is overbought. Use trailing stops.

llm.label: Parameter Tuning
llm.tooltip: Uses LLM reasoning to suggest optimal parameters for indicators and strategy rules based on recent performance.

defaultData.label: Parameter Tuning
defaultData.prompt: Based on the recent trading performance and current parameters, suggest improved parameter values to optimize the Sharpe ratio.

llm.label: Market Classification
llm.tooltip: Advanced market classification using LLM to analyze multiple factors: volatility, trend strength, volume, and macro conditions.

defaultData.label: Market Classification
defaultData.prompt: Analyze the market data and classify conditions across multiple dimensions: trend (strong/weak/none), volatility (high/medium/low), momentum (bullish/neutral/bearish).

llm.label: News → Signal
llm.tooltip: Fetches and analyzes news headlines to generate trading signals. Filters by relevance and recency.

defaultData.label: News → Signal
defaultData.prompt: Analyze the latest news for the given symbol. Determine if the news is bullish, bearish, or neutral. Consider impact magnitude and relevance.

llm.label: Custom Code
llm.tooltip: Write custom Python or JavaScript code that runs server-side. Has access to market data, indicators, and can return trading signals.

math.label: Add
math.tooltip: Add two values together. Use for combining indicators or calculating targets.

math.label: Subtract
math.tooltip: Subtract B from A. Calculate differences or spreads.

math.label: Multiply
math.tooltip: Multiply two values. Use for position sizing or ATR-based stops.

math.label: Divide
math.tooltip: Divide A by B. Calculate ratios or normalize values.

math.label: Advanced Math
math.tooltip: Apply mathematical functions: square root, absolute value, trigonometry.

math.label: Expression
math.tooltip: Evaluate a restricted Python expression over upstream node outputs. Allow-listed only: arithmetic, comparisons, `x if cond else y`, talib.<allow-list>, np.<allow-list>, abs/min/max, and the incoming-input bindings a/b/c. Parsed via AST walker — no arbitrary code, no imports, no attribute access on user objects. Safe substitute for the legacy Code node.

pineScript.label: strategy()
pineScript.tooltip: Root node for strategy scripts. Sets title, overlay, initial capital, and other strategy properties.

pineScript.label: indicator()
pineScript.tooltip: Root node for indicator-only scripts. Use this instead of strategy() when you only want overlays/plots.

pineScript.label: input.int
pineScript.tooltip: Creates a user-configurable integer input in the script settings panel.

pineScript.label: input.float
pineScript.tooltip: Creates a user-configurable decimal number input.

pineScript.label: input.bool
pineScript.tooltip: Creates a toggle checkbox input in the script settings.

pineScript.label: input.source
pineScript.tooltip: Lets user choose a price source (close, open, high, low, hl2, etc.).

pineScript.label: close
pineScript.tooltip: The closing price of each bar.

pineScript.label: open
pineScript.tooltip: The opening price of each bar.

pineScript.label: high
pineScript.tooltip: The highest price of each bar.

pineScript.label: low
pineScript.tooltip: The lowest price of each bar.

pineScript.label: volume
pineScript.tooltip: The trading volume of each bar.

pineScript.label: bar_index
pineScript.tooltip: Integer index of the current bar, starting from 0.

pineScript.label: ta.sma
pineScript.tooltip: Calculates Simple Moving Average over the specified length.

pineScript.label: ta.ema
pineScript.tooltip: Calculates Exponential Moving Average, more weight to recent prices.

pineScript.label: ta.rsi
pineScript.tooltip: Momentum oscillator measuring speed and change of price movements (0-100).

pineScript.label: ta.macd
pineScript.tooltip: Returns MACD line, signal line, and histogram as a tuple.

pineScript.label: ta.bb
pineScript.tooltip: Returns middle, upper, and lower Bollinger Bands.

pineScript.label: ta.atr
pineScript.tooltip: Measures market volatility as the average of true ranges.

pineScript.label: ta.stoch
pineScript.tooltip: Compares closing price to price range. Returns %K value.

pineScript.label: ta.vwap
pineScript.tooltip: Institutional benchmark price weighted by volume.

pineScript.label: ta.crossover
pineScript.description: Series A crosses above Series B
pineScript.tooltip: Returns true when series1 crosses above series2.

pineScript.label: ta.crossunder
pineScript.description: Series A crosses below Series B
pineScript.tooltip: Returns true when series1 crosses below series2.

pineScript.label: Compare
pineScript.tooltip: Comparison operator between two series or values.

pineScript.label: and
pineScript.tooltip: Returns true when both conditions are true.

pineScript.label: or
pineScript.tooltip: Returns true when either condition is true.

pineScript.label: not
pineScript.tooltip: Inverts a boolean value.

pineScript.label: strategy.entry
pineScript.tooltip: Opens a position. Specify id, direction, and optional quantity.

pineScript.label: strategy.close
pineScript.tooltip: Closes an existing position by its entry id.

pineScript.label: strategy.exit
pineScript.tooltip: Configures SL/TP for an existing position.

pineScript.label: plot
pineScript.description: Plot a series on the chart
pineScript.tooltip: Draws a line on the chart for a given series.

pineScript.label: plotshape
pineScript.tooltip: Draws shapes (triangles, circles, etc.) when condition is true.

pineScript.label: hline
pineScript.tooltip: Draws a fixed horizontal line at a specified price level.

pineScript.label: bgcolor
pineScript.tooltip: Colors the chart background when a condition is met.

pineScript.label: alertcondition
pineScript.tooltip: Registers a condition that can trigger TradingView alerts.

pineScript.label: alert
pineScript.tooltip: Fires an alert with a custom message when triggered.

portfolio.label: Read Holdings
portfolio.tooltip: Read all current holdings from your portfolio. Outputs a list of assets with quantities, prices, and allocations for use in strategy logic.

portfolio.label: Portfolio Value
portfolio.tooltip: Get the total current value of your portfolio in base currency. Use for position sizing relative to portfolio size.

portfolio.label: Asset Weight
portfolio.tooltip: Get the current percentage weight of a specific asset in your portfolio. Useful for rebalancing strategies.

portfolio.label: Asset P&L
portfolio.tooltip: Get the unrealized P&L for a specific holding. Outputs both absolute and percentage returns.

portfolio.label: Day Change
portfolio.tooltip: Get today\'s portfolio change in value and percentage. Useful for daily loss limit or momentum signals.

portfolio.label: Concentration Risk
portfolio.tooltip: Check if any single asset exceeds a specified allocation threshold. Returns true if portfolio is over-concentrated.

portfolio.label: Diversification Score
portfolio.tooltip: Calculate a diversification score (0-100) based on the number of asset classes, sectors, and position sizing. Higher = more diversified.

portfolio.label: Correlation Check
portfolio.tooltip: Check the correlation between two portfolio assets. High correlation means less effective diversification.

portfolio.label: Portfolio Drawdown
portfolio.tooltip: Calculate the maximum drawdown of the portfolio from its peak value. Key risk metric for portfolio health.

portfolio.label: Rebalance Signal
portfolio.tooltip: Generate a rebalance signal when any asset drifts beyond a threshold from its target allocation. Outputs which assets need buying/selling.

portfolio.label: Set Target Weight
portfolio.tooltip: Set the target percentage allocation for a specific asset. Used together with Rebalance Signal to maintain your ideal portfolio composition.

portfolio.label: Portfolio Optimizer
portfolio.tooltip: Run mean-variance optimization on your portfolio to suggest optimal weights for maximum Sharpe ratio or minimum variance.

portfolio.label: Sector Exposure
portfolio.tooltip: Break down portfolio exposure by asset class (stocks, crypto, gold, etc.). Useful for sector rotation strategies.

risk.label: Max Drawdown
risk.tooltip: Stop trading if account drops by X%. Protects capital from catastrophic losses.

risk.label: Daily Loss Limit
risk.tooltip: Stop trading for the day if losses exceed X%. Prevents revenge trading.

risk.label: Position %
risk.tooltip: Calculate position size as percentage of account. Standard risk management.

risk.label: Kelly Criterion
risk.tooltip: Calculate optimal position size based on win rate and reward/risk ratio.

risk.label: Trailing Stop %
risk.tooltip: Trailing stop that moves with price. Lock in profits automatically.

risk.label: Target Allocation
risk.tooltip: Define target percentage allocation for an asset in the portfolio. Used by rebalancing actions.

startNode.label: Start
startNode.description: Strategy entry point — portfolio, tickers, capital, mode
startNode.tooltip: The Strategy Context. Every strategy begins here. Choose the portfolio (broker account), primary tickers, starting capital, and run mode (paper / live / backtest). Downstream nodes inherit this context. There is exactly one Start node per strategy; it cannot be deleted.

tradeInfo.label: Entry Price
tradeInfo.tooltip: Get the entry price of current position. Use to calculate distance to SL/TP.

tradeInfo.label: Position Size
tradeInfo.tooltip: Get current open position size. Use for partial closes or scaling.

tradeInfo.label: Trade PnL
tradeInfo.tooltip: Get unrealized P&L of current trade. Use for trailing or time-based exits.

tradeInfo.label: Trade Duration
tradeInfo.tooltip: Get time elapsed since entry. Use for time-based exit rules.

trigger.label: Heartbeat
trigger.tooltip: Fires workflow on a configurable schedule — every N minutes, at market open/close, or daily at a specific time. Powered by BullMQ repeatable jobs. Inspired by OpenClaw heartbeat daemon.

trigger.label: Webhook
trigger.tooltip: Fires instantly when an external HTTP POST is received. Connect TradingView alerts, Zapier, or any service that sends webhooks. HMAC signature verification for security.

trigger.label: Price Alert
trigger.tooltip: Fires when a specific asset crosses a price threshold. Runs as a cheap pre-check in Node.js — no LLM or Python compute required. Extremely fast and cost-effective.

trigger.label: News Event
trigger.tooltip: Fires when a keyword or topic is detected in financial news feeds. Monitors NewsAPI, RSS, and SEC filings. Source-filtered to reduce false positives.

trigger.label: Broker Event
trigger.tooltip: Fires on broker events: order filled, margin call, position liquidated, dividend received. Connects to your linked brokerage account via the credential vault.

trigger.label: Manual Trigger
trigger.tooltip: Fires when you explicitly click "Run" in the UI or call the execute API. Inspired by n8n\'s Manual Trigger node. Useful for ad-hoc testing and one-off executions.

trigger.label: Schedule (Cron)
trigger.tooltip: Fires workflow on a precise cron schedule. Supports standard cron expressions (e.g., "30 9 * * 1-5" for weekdays at 9:30 AM). Inspired by n8n\'s Schedule Trigger node. Timezone-aware.

trigger.label: Condition Trigger
trigger.description: Fires when an incoming value meets a condition (>, <, =, etc.)
trigger.tooltip: Connect any numeric value (RSI, price, indicator) and define a threshold condition. Fires a signal downstream when satisfied. Wire an indicator output to the Value input, set the operator and threshold.

variable.label: Set Variable
variable.tooltip: Save a value to a named variable. Access it later with Get Variable.

variable.label: Get Variable
variable.tooltip: Read a previously stored variable value. Use in calculations or conditions.

variable.label: Change Variable
variable.tooltip: Increase or decrease a variable. Useful for counters and tracking.

variable.label: Number
variable.tooltip: A fixed numeric value. Use as input to other nodes.
